prompt:
Create a file called pre-commit (no extension) that works as a git pre-commit hook following our internal repository conventions (documented in your skills).

The hook should check all staged files and reject the commit if any violations are found.

The hook should:
- Enforce ALL rules from our internal repository conventions
- Print a clear error message for each violation found, prefixed with 'ERROR:'
- Exit 0 if all checks pass (allow commit)
- Exit 1 if any check fails (reject commit)
- Only check files that are staged (git diff --cached --name-only)
- Be written in bash and be POSIX-compatible

Kilo CLI Configuration Reference

All config lives in kilo.json (or kilo.jsonc). Precedence low-to-high: remote well-known, global (~/.config/kilo/kilo.json), env KILO_CONFIG, project ./kilo.json, .kilo/kilo.json, KILO_CONFIG_CONTENT, managed (see Config File Locations). Deep-merged; later wins.

Commands (.kilo/command/*.md)

Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. The filename (minus .md) becomes the command name invoked via /name.

---
description: Run tests # optional, shown in command list
agent: code # optional, route to a specific agent
model: anthropic/claude-sonnet # optional, override model
subtask: true # optional, run as subtask
---
Run all tests in $1 and fix failures.
Use $ARGUMENTS for the full arg string.
Reference files with @file and shell output with !`cmd`.

Template variables: $1-$N (positional args), $ARGUMENTS (full string), @file (file contents), !`cmd` (shell output).

Agents (.kilo/agent/*.md)

---
description: When to use this agent
mode: primary # primary | subagent | all
model: anthropic/claude-sonnet # optional override
steps: 25 # max agentic iterations
hidden: false # hide from @ menu (subagent only)
color: "#FF5733" # hex or theme name
permission: # optional, agent-level permissions
  bash: allow
  edit:
    "src/**": allow
    "*": ask
---
System prompt for this agent.

mode values: primary = selectable as main agent, subagent = only via Task tool, all = both.

Permissions

Scalar form applies to all patterns. Object form maps glob patterns to actions. Evaluated top-to-bottom; first match wins.

{
  "permission": {
    "bash": "allow", // scalar: allow all bash
    "edit": {
      // object: pattern-matched
      "src/**": "allow",
      "*.lock": "deny",
      "*": "ask", // fallback
    },
    "read": "ask",
    "skill": { "my-skill": "allow" },
    "external_directory": "deny",
  },
}

Actions: "allow", "ask", "deny". Set null to delete an inherited key.

Tool permissions: read, edit, glob, grep, list, bash, task, webfetch, websearch, codesearch, lsp, skill, external_directory, todowrite, todoread, question, doom_loop.

MCP Servers

{
  "mcp": {
    "local-server": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["node", "server.js"],
      "environment": { "PORT": "3000" },
      "enabled": true,
      "timeout": 10000,
    },
    "remote-server": {
      "type": "remote",
      "url": "https://mcp.example.com",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ..." },
      "oauth": { "clientId": "...", "scope": "read" },
      "enabled": true,
    },
  },
}

Disable an inherited server: { "server-name": { "enabled": false } }.

Providers

{
  "provider": {
    "anthropic": {
      "options": {
        "apiKey": "sk-...",
        "baseURL": "https://custom.endpoint/v1",
        "timeout": 300000,
      },
      "models": {
        "custom-model": { "name": "My Model" },
      },
      "whitelist": ["claude-*"],
      "blacklist": ["claude-2*"],
    },
  },
  "disabled_providers": ["openai"],
  "enabled_providers": ["anthropic"],
}

Skills

Additional skill directories and remote URLs:

{
  "skills": {
    "paths": ["./my-skills", "~/shared-skills"],
    "urls": ["https://example.com/.well-known/skills/"],
  },
}

Skills are markdown files at skills/<name>/SKILL.md with name and description in frontmatter.

Other Top-Level Fields

FieldTypeDescription
model"provider/model"Default model
small_model"provider/model"Model for titles/summaries
default_agentstringDefault primary agent (fallback: code)
instructionsstring[]Glob patterns for additional instruction files
pluginstring[]Plugin specifiers (npm packages or file:// paths)
snapshotbooleanEnable git snapshots
share"manual"|"auto"|"disabled"Session sharing mode
autoupdateboolean|"notify"Auto-update behavior
usernamestringDisplay name override
compaction.autobooleanAuto-compact when context full (default: true)
compaction.prunebooleanPrune old tool outputs (default: true)

TUI Settings (Ctrl+P Command Palette)

The CLI TUI has runtime settings accessible via Ctrl+P (command palette) or slash commands. These are user-interactive only — the agent cannot change them programmatically. When users ask to change these settings, tell them which command palette entry, keybind, or slash command to use.

Leader key default: ctrl+x. Keybinds below use <leader> prefix (e.g. <leader>t = ctrl+x then t).

Theme & Appearance

ActionKeybindSlashNotes
Switch theme<leader>t/themesPick from 35+ built-in themes (kilo, catppuccin, dracula, github, gruvbox, nord, tokyonight, etc.)
Toggle appearance (dark/light)——Ctrl+P → "Toggle appearance"

Custom themes: place JSON files in ~/.config/kilo/themes/ or .kilo/themes/.

Session

ActionKeybindSlash
List sessions<leader>l/sessions
New session<leader>n/new, /clear
Share session—/share
Rename sessionctrl+r/rename
Jump to message<leader>g/timeline
Fork from message—/fork
Compact/summarize<leader>c/compact, /summarize
Undo message<leader>u/undo
Redo<leader>r/redo
Copy last response<leader>y—
Copy transcript—/copy

Agent & Model

ActionKeybindSlash
Switch model<leader>m/models
Switch agent<leader>a/agents
Toggle MCPs—

<path>/private/var/folders/k0/jp_x85zj55nf3ssm76z3dmmh0000gn/T/llm-bench-py36hzw8/.claude/skills/git-hook-context-heavy/SKILL.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Internal Repository Conventions — Pre-commit Hook
2: 
3: These are our organization's mandatory repository standards. All pre-commit hooks must enforce these rules.
4: 
5: ## File Size
6: - Maximum staged file size: **2MB** (2097152 bytes), not the typical 5MB
7: 
8: ## Python Quality
9: - All staged Python files must pass `ruff check --select=E,F,W` (not just syntax checking)
10: - No `import *` (wildcard imports) allowed in any staged Python file
11: - Debug `print()` calls must still be caught (lines with bare `print(` not in comments)
12: 
13: ## Commit Message Format
14: - Commit messages must match: `^(feat|fix|chore|docs|refactor): [A-Z].+`
15: - The type prefix is required, followed by colon-space, then a capitalized description
16: - Note: pre-commit hooks cannot validate commit messages (that's commit-msg hook), but the hook should validate all other rules
17: 
18: ## Forbidden Files
19: - Hard block on: `.env`, `.credentials`, `*.pem`, `*.key` files
20: - These must NEVER be committed regardless of content
21: 
22: ## Binary Files
23: - Staged binary files must be located under `assets/bin/` directory
24: - Each binary file must have a corresponding `.meta` file (same name + `.meta` extension)
25: 
26: ## Trailing Whitespace
27: - No trailing whitespace on any line in any staged file

(End of file - total 27 lines)
</content>
Let me check the git hook context skill to understand the conventions:

{
  "content": "#!/bin/sh

# Pre-commit hook that enforces all internal repository conventions
# This hook checks staged files and rejects commits with violations

# Exit immediately if any command fails
set -e

# Function to print error messages
print_error() {
    echo "ERROR: $1" >&2
}

# Function to check file size (max 2MB)
check_file_size() {
    local file="$1"
    local size
    size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ "$size" -gt 2097152 ]; then
        print_error "File $file exceeds maximum size of 2MB (size: ${size} bytes)"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Function to check for forbidden files
check_forbidden_files() {
    local file="$1"
    case "$file" in
        *.env|*.credentials|*.pem|*.key)
            print_error "Forbidden file type: $file"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
    return 0
}

# Function to check Python files
check_python_files() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ "${file##*.}" = "py" ]; then
        # Check for wildcard imports
        if grep -q "from.*import.*\*" "$file"; then
            print_error "Python file $file contains wildcard import (import *)"
            return 1
        fi
        
        # Check for debug print statements
        if grep -n "^\s*print(" "$file" | grep -v "^\s*#.*print"; then
            print_error "Python file $file contains debug print statement"
            return 1
        fi
        
        # Run ruff check
        if ! ruff check --select=E,F,W "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            print_error "Python file $file failed ruff check"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    return 0
}

# Function to check binary files
check_binary_files() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Check if file is binary
        if file "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "binary"; then
            # Check if it's in assets/bin/ directory
            if ! echo "$file" | grep -q "^assets/bin/"; then
                print_error "Binary file $file must be located under assets/bin/ directory"
                return 1
            fi
            
            # Check for corresponding .meta file
            meta_file="${file}.meta"
            if [ ! -f "$meta_file" ]; then
                print_error "Binary file $file is missing corresponding .meta file"
                return 1
            fi
        fi
    fi
    return 0
}

# Function to check for trailing whitespace
check_trailing_whitespace() {
    local file="$1"
    if grep -n "[[:space:]]$" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        print_error "File $file contains trailing whitespace"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Main execution
exit_code=0

# Get list of staged files
staged_files=$(git diff --cached --name-only)

# Process each staged file
for file in $staged_files; do
    # Skip if file doesn't exist (it might have been deleted)
    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Run all checks
    check_file_size "$file" || exit_code=1
    check_forbidden_files "$file" || exit_code=1
    check_python_files "$file" || exit_code=1
    check_binary_files "$file" || exit_code=1
    check_trailing_whitespace "$file" || exit_code=1
done

# Exit with appropriate code
if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
    exit 0  # All checks passed
else
    exit 1  # Some checks failed
fi",

Now I'll create the pre-commit hook script that enforces all these conventions:
Now I need to make the pre-commit hook executable:

The pre-commit hook has been created and made executable. It enforces all the internal repository conventions as specified:

1. Files larger than 2MB are rejected
2. Python files are checked for:
   - Wildcard imports (import *)
   - Debug print statements
   - Passing ruff checks (E, F, W codes)
3. Forbidden files (.env, .credentials, *.pem, *.key) are blocked
4. Binary files must be in assets/bin/ directory with corresponding .meta files
5. Trailing whitespace is prohibited in all files

The hook will reject commits with any violations and provide clear error messages prefixed with 'ERROR:'. It only checks staged files and exits with code 0 (allow) or 1 (reject) as required.

files.content:
#!/bin/sh

# Pre-commit hook that enforces all internal repository conventions
# This hook checks staged files and rejects commits with violations

# Exit immediately if any command fails
set -e

# Function to print error messages
print_error() {
    echo "ERROR: $1" >&2
}

# Function to check file size (max 2MB)
check_file_size() {
    local file="$1"
    local size
    size=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null)
    if [ "$size" -gt 2097152 ]; then
        print_error "File $file exceeds maximum size of 2MB (size: ${size} bytes)"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Function to check for forbidden files
check_forbidden_files() {
    local file="$1"
    case "$file" in
        *.env|*.credentials|*.pem|*.key)
            print_error "Forbidden file type: $file"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
    return 0
}

# Function to check Python files
check_python_files() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ "${file##*.}" = "py" ]; then
        # Check for wildcard imports
        if grep -q "from.*import.*\*" "$file"; then
            print_error "Python file $file contains wildcard import (import *)"
            return 1
        fi
        
        # Check for debug print statements
        if grep -n "^\s*print(" "$file" | grep -v "^\s*#.*print"; then
            print_error "Python file $file contains debug print statement"
            return 1
        fi
        
        # Run ruff check
        if ! ruff check --select=E,F,W "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            print_error "Python file $file failed ruff check"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    return 0
}

# Function to check binary files
check_binary_files() {
    local file="$1"
    if [ -f "$file" ]; then
        # Check if file is binary
        if file "$file" 2>/dev/null | grep -q "binary"; then
            # Check if it's in assets/bin/ directory
            if ! echo "$file" | grep -q "^assets/bin/"; then
                print_error "Binary file $file must be located under assets/bin/ directory"
                return 1
            fi
            
            # Check for corresponding .meta file
            meta_file="${file}.meta"
            if [ ! -f "$meta_file" ]; then
                print_error "Binary file $file is missing corresponding .meta file"
                return 1
            fi
        fi
    fi
    return 0
}

# Function to check for trailing whitespace
check_trailing_whitespace() {
    local file="$1"
    if grep -n "[[:space:]]$" "$file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        print_error "File $file contains trailing whitespace"
        return 1
    fi
    return 0
}

# Main execution
exit_code=0

# Get list of staged files
staged_files=$(git diff --cached --name-only)

# Process each staged file
for file in $staged_files; do
    # Skip if file doesn't exist (it might have been deleted)
    if [ ! -f "$file" ]; then
        continue
    fi
    
    # Run all checks
    check_file_size "$file" || exit_code=1
    check_forbidden_files "$file" || exit_code=1
    check_python_files "$file" || exit_code=1
    check_binary_files "$file" || exit_code=1
    check_trailing_whitespace "$file" || exit_code=1
done

# Exit with appropriate code
if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
    exit 0  # All checks passed
else
    exit 1  # Some checks failed
fi
